title: Discourje: Run-Time Verification of Communication Protocols in Clojure -- Live at Last (Artifact)
author: Jongmans, Sung-Shik
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Multiparty session typing (MPST) is a formal method to make concurrent programming simpler. The idea is to use type checking to automatically prove safety (protocol compliance) and liveness (communication deadlock freedom) of implementations relative to specifications. Discourje is an existing run-time verification library for communication protocols in Clojure, based on dynamic MPST. The original version of Discourje can detect only safety violations. In the paper "Discourje: Run-Time Verification of Communication Protocols in Clojure -- Live at Last" (FM'24), we present an extension of Discourje to detect also liveness violations. 
 
 This artifact contains the new version of Discourje, including the examples of the paper.
steps_raw:
Multiparty session typing (MPST) is a formal method to make concurrent programming simpler. The idea is to use type checking to automatically prove safety (protocol compliance) and liveness (communication deadlock freedom) of implementations relative to specifications. Discourje is an existing run-time verification library for communication protocols in Clojure, based on dynamic MPST. The original version of Discourje can detect only safety violations. In the paper "Discourje: Run-Time Verification of Communication Protocols in Clojure -- Live at Last" (FM'24), we present an extension of Discourje to detect also liveness violations. 
 
 This artifact contains the new version of Discourje, including the examples of the paper.
